qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Embedded admin server is enabled by default and exposes unauthenticated command endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unauthenticated HTTP admin commands in AdminServerFactory, JettyAdminServer, and Commands, while the advisory is a ZKConfig logging flaw that exposes client configuration values in client logs.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's ZKConfig configuration-value logging root cause; the only final qualifying finding is a distinct unauthenticated admin-interface exposure.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes improper handling of configuration values in org.apache.zookeeper.common.ZKConfig that exposes sensitive client configuration values in the client's logfile at INFO level.
In ZooKeeper 3.9.4, ZKConfig(File) logs the full properties map with LOG.info("ZK Config {}", this.properties); in 3.9.5 the same constructor first redacts sensitive values before logging.

The only final qualifying finding's code locations are zookeeper-server/src/main/java/org/apache/zookeeper/server/admin/AdminServerFactory.java, JettyAdminServer.java, Commands.java, and ZooKeeperServerMain.java, and its exploit path is unauthenticated HTTP access to /commands/*.
